message:
fix: don't abort FTS rebuild when a batch inserts zero rows

rebuild_fts_via_frankensqlite drives batching with a rowid-bounded INSERT
loop, and the loop used 'if inserted == 0 { break }' as an early
termination.  That's wrong whenever a batch range contains only orphaned
messages — e.g. rows whose conversation_id no longer resolves because of
a partial delete or crash-truncated import.  The INNER JOIN on
conversations drops such rows, inserted comes back as 0, and the loop
breaks with valid messages beyond the orphaned range left unindexed.

Termination should be driven solely by batch_max_rowid returning None,
which happens exactly when we have scanned past the last message.  With
the early break removed, a zero-insert batch simply advances last_rowid
to the queried upper bound and continues processing subsequent batches.

This bug was masked before 860acb12 because the then-current 3-table
INNER JOIN required matching rows in both conversations and agents, and
a whole batch missing agents is rare.  After 860acb12 promoted agent
lookup to a correlated subquery (and e1c08e7c added the 'unknown'
fallback), only dangling conversation_id still causes a zero-insert
batch — but the early-termination bug was always latent.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
